assertions.text: Picks ft(corpus): prefix for knowledge/idioms/* write
No conventional-commits prefix (feat:/fix:/chore:)
Title is imperative present-tense with no trailing period
Title parenthetical includes line count + cite count per §Body rules
Body explains the WHY (not a diff recap)
Body wrapped near 76 cols
Includes Co-authored-by trailer
Co-authored-by uses lowercase form (Co-authored-by:) not GitHub-style uppercase (Co-Authored-By:)
Co-authored-by identity is exactly 'Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>'
No Plan: trailer (no plan.md backs this commit)
No Signed-off-by trailer

evals.note: A8 fails with_skill in iter-1 because SKILL.md itself says 'Co-Authored-By:' (uppercase) in 3 places — but the real meta-repo log uses lowercase (45 lowercase vs 11 uppercase in last 50 commits). The with_skill answer faithfully followed the SKILL.md spelling. This is the highest-leverage edit candidate.

assertions.text: Picks ft(skills): or ft(meta): prefix (not feat:)
Plan: trailer present
Plan: trailer matches exact format 'planning/<slug>/plan.md (phase <N>: <title>)'
Sites: trailer present and lists each non-obvious file
Trailers separated from body by a blank line
Plan: trailer ordered before Co-authored-by (Co-authored-by is last)
Co-authored-by trailer present (last)
Co-authored-by lowercase form
Single commit (not split — these are all meta-repo writes; R10 split rule applies only when one change touches BOTH dev/ and meta)
Picks plural scope ft(skills) matching real log (23 occurrences) rather than singular ft(skill) from the SKILL.md example
assertions.note: with_skill caught the singular/plural drift between the SKILL.md example and the real log — bonus point for the agent.

assertions.text: Explicitly rejects feat(skills): as wrong prefix (conventional-commits forbidden)
Explicitly rejects bare feat: with no scope (scope is mandatory)
Explicitly rejects bare-imperative title with no prefix (for meta repo)
States the bare-imperative form IS correct for upstream PG / dev/ (commit-message-style)
Canonical form named as ft(scope): with mandatory scope
Co-authored-by trailer required for the corrected form
Co-authored-by lowercase form named
No Signed-off-by (not a DCO project)
Mentions ticket numbers are forbidden inline (squash-merge appends (#NN) automatically)
Names the contrast skill commit-message-style for upstream PG style

notes: Strongest signal: SKILL.md itself uses Co-Authored-By: (GitHub uppercase) in every example, but the actual meta-repo log uses lowercase Co-authored-by: 45 times vs 11 uppercase in the most-recent ~50 commits with a co-author trailer. This is the highest-leverage fix — the skill teaches an incorrect spelling.

Singular/plural drift: SKILL.md example uses ft(skill): (singular), real log uses ft(skills): (plural, 23 occurrences). Same for the scope vocabulary — real log has ft(meta), docs(state), docs(cloud), docs(community), ft(patches), ft(ideologies) — broader than what the skill enumerates.

Plan: trailer in the wild appears NOT just for planning/<slug>/plan.md (phase N: title) but for looser things like 'Plan: cloud routine pg-quality-auditor (AUDIT mode, 2026-06-14)'. Skill should either tighten the format to be authoritative or acknowledge the looser usage.

[cloud:<routine>] form in real log appears with descriptive payload (e.g. '5 commits · 8 bf failures') not a colon-then-imperative title. Skill example is structurally accurate but worth a real-log anchor.
Baseline reliably reaches for Conventional Commits + uppercase Co-Authored-By: Claude — both are wrong for this repo and the skill cleanly steers away from both (except for the casing bug).
